name: Turbo
description: Rust-first retro/fantasy-console-style game engine targeting fast iteration and WASM

kind.comment: Self-described 'command-line based game engine' for low-res 2D games; in Lineup's taxonomy it's a code-first framework (no visual editor or scene model — you write a Rust update/render loop). Fantasy-console/retro style with an online backend.

license.comment: Public Rust SDK (turbo-genesis-sdk) is MIT-licensed on crates.io. The core engine was historically a private repo under AGPL-3.0 (per FAQ); AGPL-3.0 has no tag in this schema, but the developer-facing SDK that users depend on is MIT.

repository.comment: Public Rust SDK repo. The engine runtime lives in turbo-cli and a separate (historically private) core engine repo; org also hosts turbo-demos.

crates-io.comment: Top-level Rust crate used by games. The engine/CLI itself is distributed via an install script (curl | sh), not crates.io.

first-release.comment: turbo-genesis-sdk crate first published 2023-08-19 on crates.io.

latest-stable-version.comment: Project is in alpha — no truly stable release. turbo-cli latest is 0.10.0-alpha.0 (2026-01-05); turbo-genesis-sdk max stable 5.2.1, newest 6.0.0-alpha.0. Checked 2026-07-06.

maintenance-status.comment: Public repos (turbo-cli, turbo-genesis-sdk) last updated 2026-01-05, ~6 months before this check (2026-07-06). Core engine development happens in a separate/private repo, so public commit cadence may understate real activity.

maturity.comment: Explicitly alpha (all releases are pre-1.0 alphas); API still churning, no widely-documented commercial titles. 3D support still planned.

backing.comment: Developed by Super Turbo Society, a small startup (teams in New York and Massachusetts) with optional Solana blockchain integration.

implementation.comment: Engine and SDK are written in Rust; games compile to WebAssembly and run on a Rust/WebGPU runtime. Not a binding to a non-Rust engine.

architecture.comment: Immediate-mode style: a persistent, auto-serialized game-state struct plus a per-frame update/render function that redraws from state. No ECS or scene graph.

built-in-ecs.comment: State is a single serialized struct + update loop, not an entity-component-system.

dimensionality.comment: Quad-based pipeline focused solely on low-res 2D games; 3D is planned but not available.

scripting.comment: Rust is the only officially supported language today; additional languages are planned.

data-driven-scenes.comment: No serialized scene/prefab file format — games are authored in code; only sprite/font/audio/shader assets live in files.

reflection-serialization.comment: The game-state struct is automatically serialized (persisted across frames and hot reloads, and enabling headless server-side state verification).

hot-reload.comment: Live coding: games hot-reload whenever code, shaders, or sprites are modified — a headline feature.

visual-editor.comment: Command-line based; no graphical scene/level editor.

live-inspector.comment: No documented runtime world/state inspector UI.

asset-pipeline.comment: Assets organized in sprites/fonts/audio/shaders directories with async loading and hot reload, but no reprocessing/import pipeline — rates ~3.

cli-project-tooling.comment: Dedicated `turbo` CLI (init/run/export) that scaffolds projects from starter templates.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: Console logging is available via the system module, but no dedicated profiler, Tracy integration, or egui overlay is documented.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer works as usual); no dedicated IDE integration.

rendering-backend.comment: Powered by WebAssembly + WebGPU; the Rust runtime uses wgpu so it also runs natively on desktop. Quad-based render pipeline.

lighting.comment: No first-party lighting system documented (low-res 2D drawing primitives).

custom-shaders.comment: Shaders are a first-class asset type (dedicated shaders directory, hot-reloaded); users author custom WGSL shaders.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: First-class sprite support (static and animated) with batching via the quad pipeline. No dedicated first-party tilemap API found — tilemaps would be hand-built from sprites.

post-processing.comment: No named built-in post-processing stack (bloom/tonemapping/TAA); custom shaders exist but there is no documented post-process pipeline.

text-rendering.comment: Built-in text rendering with font assets, plus text boxes and nine-slice helpers.

physics.comment: No physics engine; only bounds/collision helper utilities are provided — physics is bring-your-own.

audio.comment: Built-in audio module: load/play/loop audio assets (music and sounds).

input.comment: Input module handles keyboard, mouse, touch, and gamepad; the pointer API unifies mouse and touch.

animation.comment: Animated sprites (frame-based) plus built-in tweening/easing utilities. No skeletal animation (2D engine).

ui-system.comment: Built-in immediate-mode GUI helpers: advanced layout utilities, nine-slices, and text boxes.

networking.comment: First-class online multiplayer via 'Turbo OS' backend (network channels/commands, leaderboards); games can run headlessly for server-side state verification. Optional Solana integration.

windows.comment: Windows supported (docs recommend running the installer via Git Bash as administrator).

web-wasm.comment: Compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown; easy web publishing is a headline feature.

ios.comment: No documented native iOS export (desktop + web only). Games can run in mobile browsers via the web build.

android.comment: No documented native Android export (desktop + web only). Games can run in mobile browsers via the web build.

clean-build-time.comment: Not measured on a stated machine. Turbo advertises fast iteration and small builds, but no reliable clean-build number was verified.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured. Live-coding hot reload recompiles only the small game WASM crate and is advertised as near-instant, but no measured rebuild time was verified.

binary-size.comment: Not measured for the native runtime; no reliable stripped-binary figure verified.

web-bundle-size.comment: Marketing claims 'small build sizes' for web export, but no measured post-wasm-opt bundle size was verified.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not measured; prefer null over a guess for idle CPU/memory of a trivial app.

github-stars.comment: turbo-genesis-sdk repo had 10 stars on 2026-07-06. Star count is fragmented and understates reach: the core engine repo is separate/private, turbo-cli has ~4 stars, and turbo-demos has ~41.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of turbo-genesis-sdk as of 2026-07-06.

shipped-titles.comment: No widely-documented commercially released titles; usage is mostly official demos and game-jam projects.

community-size.comment: Small but active community (Discord, small startup team, few external contributors on the public repos).

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: Young project; no third-party plugin/asset registry — batteries-included core with little external ecosystem.

learning-resources.comment: Official docs site with getting-started, tutorials, a cheatsheet, video tutorials, and a demos repo — solid guides and examples, but no full-length book. Rates ~3.
